title: foundation — cache tag namespaces + base keys
description: Foundation reserves two cache tag namespaces (`platform`, `foundation`) so no two modules collide. Downstream modules use their own module-name tags (`tenants`, `invitations`, ...). Every cache entry in every module MUST be tagged; untagged writes are refused by the base repository.

reservedTags.description: Cross-module platform state — module registry, health snapshot, config-reload signal. Owned by foundation.
Foundation-internal state — search engine health, permission seeding checksum. Owned by foundation.

downstreamConvention.rule: Every downstream module uses its module name as a tag: `tenants`, `invitations`, `notifications`, `user`, etc. Composite tags allowed (`invitations`, `tenant`) for tenant-scoped tenant-owned data.
downstreamConvention.enforcement: BaseRepository refuses `cache()->put()` calls without a tag list. Tags array is inspected against a checksum of every module's caches.json at boot — unknown tags fail the build.

keys.description: Materialised ModuleRegistry state — sorted by dependency + probe registrations. Warmed at boot, invalidated on ConfigReloaded.
Aggregate health status. Written every 5m by foundation:health-snapshot + read by every /api/health call. Cached for 5s at the read side to smooth request bursts.
Search engine reachable ping result. Prevents every Searchable-model save from re-pinging the engine.
Hash of every module's permissions.json — used to detect seed drift between deploys.
